evidence_quotes.quote: Video games is a huge industry, with very little and in fact diminishing ownership rights for consumers.
license that can be ripped out from under your feet

paragraphs.text: Good day! At a time with evolving technology, policy seems to often be lagging behind as it relates to consumer rights. So this Act has a thumbs up from me, an average joe, but. It could be even better! Video games is a huge industry, with very little and in fact diminishing ownership rights for consumers. We are seeing a pattern of exploitative EULA's and practices done by a very rich and influential industry. Consumers have online products ripped out from in between their fingers, because big industries plan ahead poorly and most importantly, greedily. As a EU adjacent consumer, I would very much like to see consumer rights translate to online purchases. As if right now, giant multi million publishers are acting as if they do not owe the consumer anything. In fact, they take purchases away and shut them down forever, despite the fact that most consumers ASSUME a purchase = ownership. It is not stated clearly anywhere that it is in fact most of the time just a license that can be ripped out from under your feet, 'for no reason'. Imagine if a car manufacturer showed up at your house one year after your purchase; to take away your steering wheel because they no longer support the model! A crude comparison perhaps but it is what is happening to consumers paid for products. Digital Fairness, to me, means a right to be informed. If a product is not what the common consumer generally or overall assumes, then this must be stated clearly, front and center of the product. We don't have this right now.
